title: prunus subcordata subsp. rubicunda
intro: Prunus subcordata subsp. rubicunda, also known as the Rubicunda plum, is a subspecies of the western sand cherry, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The Rubicunda plum is also valued for its potential health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels and aiding in the treatment of cardiovascular disease, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Prunus subcordata subsp. rubicunda include tea and tincture, which can be made by infusing the dried fruit or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
